questions.q: Devika Rani is often honoured with which famous title in the history of Indian cinema?
questions.explanation: Devika Rani, a pioneering actress of early sound cinema, is widely remembered as the 'First Lady of Indian Cinema'.

questions.q: Along with her husband Himanshu Rai, Devika Rani co-founded which famous film studio in 1934?
questions.explanation: Devika Rani and Himanshu Rai co-founded Bombay Talkies in 1934, one of the most influential studios of early Indian cinema.

questions.q: In which year did Devika Rani become the first recipient of the Dadasaheb Phalke Award?
questions.explanation: Devika Rani was honoured as the first-ever recipient of the Dadasaheb Phalke Award in 1969.

questions.q: Who was Devika Rani's husband and co-founder of Bombay Talkies?
questions.explanation: Devika Rani was married to actor-producer Himanshu Rai, with whom she co-founded Bombay Talkies.

questions.q: In which year was Devika Rani born?
questions.explanation: Devika Rani was born in 1908.

questions.q: The 1936 film Achhut Kanya, starring Devika Rani, dealt with which prominent social theme?
questions.explanation: Achhut Kanya (1936) addressed the theme of untouchability through the love story of a Brahmin boy and an untouchable girl played by Devika Rani.

questions.q: Devika Rani was a leading star associated primarily with which era of Indian films?
questions.explanation: Devika Rani was a leading actress of early sound cinema in the 1930s and 1940s.

questions.q: The Dadasaheb Phalke Award, first given to Devika Rani, is conferred for excellence in which field?
questions.explanation: The Dadasaheb Phalke Award is India's highest honour for lifetime contribution to Indian cinema.

questions.q: In which year did Devika Rani pass away?
questions.explanation: Devika Rani died in 1994.

questions.q: Devika Rani made her notable screen debut in which 1933 film?
questions.explanation: Devika Rani starred in Karma (1933), an early Indian English-language sound film made with Himanshu Rai.

questions.q: The 1933 film Karma, starring Devika Rani, was notable for being made largely in which language?
questions.explanation: Karma (1933) was a bilingual production made primarily in English, aimed at international audiences.

questions.q: Devika Rani's famously long on-screen kiss, often cited as one of the longest in Indian cinema, appeared in which 1933 film?
questions.explanation: Karma (1933) featured a prolonged kissing scene between Devika Rani and Himanshu Rai, widely noted in cinema history.

questions.q: In Achhut Kanya (1936), Devika Rani was paired opposite which leading actor of Bombay Talkies?
questions.explanation: Devika Rani starred opposite Ashok Kumar in Achhut Kanya (1936), one of their celebrated pairings at Bombay Talkies.

questions.q: Which famous Bombay Talkies leading man, who later became a legendary star, was discovered and promoted during Devika Rani's time at the studio?
questions.explanation: Dilip Kumar was given his screen name and launched by Bombay Talkies under Devika Rani's leadership in the 1940s.

questions.q: Devika Rani had a notable family connection to which Nobel laureate?
questions.explanation: Devika Rani was a grand-niece of the poet Rabindranath Tagore, India's first Nobel laureate in literature.

questions.q: Before her film career, Devika Rani received training in which country, including study at the Royal Academy of Dramatic Art?
questions.explanation: Devika Rani studied in England, including training at the Royal Academy of Dramatic Art in London.

questions.q: After Himanshu Rai's death in 1940, Devika Rani took on which role at Bombay Talkies?
options: She helped run and produce films at the studio
She joined a rival studio as an actress only
questions.explanation: Following Himanshu Rai's death in 1940, Devika Rani continued to lead and produce films at Bombay Talkies.

questions.q: Devika Rani's second husband was which celebrated Russian-born painter?
questions.explanation: After leaving films, Devika Rani married the Russian painter Svetoslav Roerich and settled near Bangalore.

questions.q: In 1958, Devika Rani became one of the early recipients of which civilian honour conferred by the Government of India?
questions.explanation: Devika Rani was awarded the Padma Shri by the Government of India in 1958 for her contribution to Indian cinema. The Param Vir Chakra and Ashoka Chakra are military gallantry awards, and she was never given the Bharat Ratna.

questions.q: The song 'Main Ban Ki Chidiya', a popular early film song, featured Devika Rani in which 1936 film?
questions.explanation: The duet 'Main Ban Ki Chidiya' was picturised on Devika Rani and Ashok Kumar in Achhut Kanya (1936).

questions.q: Bombay Talkies, co-founded by Devika Rani, established its studio in which suburb of Bombay (now Mumbai)?
questions.explanation: Bombay Talkies built its studio in Malad in the northern suburbs of Bombay, where films like Achhut Kanya were produced.

questions.q: In which decade did Devika Rani retire from acting and the film industry?
questions.explanation: Devika Rani retired from films in the 1940s after marrying Svetoslav Roerich.

questions.q: Devika Rani's film debut Karma (1933) was directed by which filmmaker?
questions.explanation: Karma (1933) was directed by the British filmmaker J. L. Freer Hunt.

questions.q: Which German director, associated with Bombay Talkies, directed several of Devika Rani's films including Achhut Kanya?
questions.explanation: Franz Osten, a German director, made many Bombay Talkies films starring Devika Rani, including Achhut Kanya (1936).

questions.q: What was the title of the 1936 Bombay Talkies film in which Devika Rani and Ashok Kumar first appeared together as a screen pair before Achhut Kanya?
questions.explanation: Devika Rani and Ashok Kumar were first paired in Jeevan Naiya (1936) before their success in Achhut Kanya.

questions.q: Devika Rani was born in which present-day Indian state?
questions.explanation: Devika Rani was born in Waltair (Visakhapatnam) in present-day Andhra Pradesh.

questions.q: Which acclaimed music director worked at Bombay Talkies and composed music for films during the era associated with Devika Rani?
questions.explanation: Saraswati Devi was the music director at Bombay Talkies, composing for films of the Devika Rani era including Achhut Kanya.

questions.q: In which 1935 Bombay Talkies film, considered a daring social drama, did Devika Rani star early in her career?
questions.explanation: Jawani Ki Hawa (1935) was an early Bombay Talkies film starring Devika Rani.

questions.q: Devika Rani spent her later life at an estate near Bangalore with Svetoslav Roerich, which is today preserved as which kind of site?
options: An art gallery and nature reserve / estate
questions.explanation: Devika Rani and Svetoslav Roerich lived at the Tataguni estate near Bangalore, later preserved for its art and natural heritage.

questions.q: In 2011, India Post honoured Devika Rani in which way to mark her contribution to cinema?
options: By naming a film award after her
By building a memorial cinema hall
By issuing a commemorative postage stamp
questions.explanation: India Post issued a commemorative postage stamp honouring Devika Rani as part of a series on Indian cinema legends.
